detail: GENUINE incomplete fix. The FA-0584 fix (commit c1124d2) correctly fixed the ORIGINAL defect (PATCH now runs _validate_normalize_schedule before persisting schedule_json). But it ALSO bundled a brand-new PATCH-writable week_overrides block, and that addition is incomplete. Git confirms the week_overrides PATCH block at runtime_views.py:1389-1395 did not exist before c1124d2 (week_overrides was not a writable PATCH field). The block validates overrides via _validate_normalize_week_overrides, assigns plan.week_overrides, sets needs_regeneration=True, and returns HTTP 200 -- but the regen path it triggers cannot consume week_overrides. At runtime_views.py:1431-1433 PATCH calls reconcile_plan_state(plan, plan.schedule_json, plan.weeks, today=...) plus apply_reconciliation, neither of which receives or reads week_overrides. Confirmed by grep: apps/fuel/services.py has ZERO references to week_overrides. The reconciler builds ONE weekday-keyed template via _parse_schedule_template(schedule_json) (services.py:127) and applies it identically to every week (for w in range(weeks) at services.py:139; template_by_weekday.get(...) at lines 203 and 250) -- there is no week dimension, so per-week deloads (different detail/category/rpe for week N) and per-week rest-day drops (a day mapped to null) are structurally inexpressible on this path. Only the POST/create path honors them, via _expand_plan_workouts(..., week_overrides=normalized_overrides) at runtime_views.py:1284 (per-week merge plus null-day drop at lines 1050-1059). Net effect: create-with-overrides materializes correctly; PATCH-with-overrides stores AND round-trips the overrides (_serialize_plan includes week_overrides at line 872) and reports 200 success, but the materialized Workout calendar is unchanged. The fix disposition advertises week_overrides validation for POST parity, which is a half-truth: it delivers VALIDATION parity but NOT MATERIALIZATION parity. The claim's empirical repro (PATCH 200, overrides stored, but Week2-Mon still Heavy Squats rpe None instead of Deload Squats rpe 5, and the rest-day Wed not dropped) is fully consistent with the code. USER/OPERATOR IMPACT: this is a runtime/OpenClaw assistant-facing endpoint. A user asking the assistant to make week 3 a deload or add a rest day to an existing periodized plan via PATCH gets a 200 success and a confirmation, but the calendar silently does not change -- a real, user-reachable data-integrity/UX defect, though bounded (periodized-plan edits only, no crash, no security impact). The only test (tests.py:3669 test_week_overrides_progression_and_rest) exercises POST only; no PATCH-materialization coverage exists.
suggested_fix: Thread week_overrides through the reconcile path so PATCH materialization matches POST. Concretely: (1) add a week_overrides param to reconcile_plan_state and have it select the effective per-week template (base schedule merged with week_overrides[str(week_offset)], dropping days mapped to null) when computing desired_keys, workouts_to_create, and workouts_to_retemplate -- i.e. resolve template per (week_index, weekday) rather than per weekday only; (2) pass plan.week_overrides from the PATCH call site at runtime_views.py:1431-1433; (3) ensure rest-day drops (null) archive the corresponding slot/workout for that specific week. Alternatively, if the reconciler is too costly to make week-aware, have the PATCH path fall back to the POST-style _expand_plan_workouts regeneration whenever week_overrides is present (accepting the slot-identity churn trade-off). Add a PATCH-materialization test mirroring tests.py:3669 (PATCH week_overrides, assert calendar dates and the deload activity/rpe).
evidence: apps/fuel/runtime_views.py:1389-1395 -- new PATCH week_overrides block validates+stores plan.week_overrides, sets needs_regeneration=True
apps/fuel/runtime_views.py:1431-1433 -- regen calls reconcile_plan_state(plan, plan.schedule_json, plan.weeks, today=...); week_overrides never passed
apps/fuel/services.py grep -- ZERO references to week_overrides in reconcile_plan_state / apply_reconciliation
apps/fuel/services.py:127,139,203,250 -- _parse_schedule_template builds one weekday-keyed template applied identically across every week; no week dimension
apps/fuel/runtime_views.py:1284 + 1050-1059 -- only the POST path (_expand_plan_workouts) applies per-week overrides and drops null days
apps/fuel/runtime_views.py:872 -- _serialize_plan returns week_overrides, so PATCH round-trips them despite no materialization
git show c1124d2 -- the week_overrides PATCH block is newly added by the fix; before c1124d2 PATCH had no week_overrides handling
apps/fuel/tests.py:3669 -- sole week_overrides test uses POST (_post) only; no PATCH-materialization coverage
